circularity_caveat: The Fixsen 2009 reference temperature is itself derived by fitting a Planck spectrum to FIRAS, so this is a blackbody self-consistency check in scope, not independent empirical validation of Wien displacement.

definitions.absolute_product_gate: fail if the committed product is residual-only/model-normalized
definitions.frequency_domain_peak_distinct: the native frequency-domain peak must differ from the wavelength-domain peak by more than one bin
definitions.no_jacobian_relabel_rejected: relabelling wavenumber as wavelength without the Jacobian must miss the reference by at least the control threshold
definitions.wrong_temperature_rejected: an intentionally offset reference temperature (+10%) must miss

domain_conversion.jacobian: B_lambda = B_nu * nu^2 / c (nu = c * k_m, lambda = 1 / k_m)
domain_conversion.note: Frequency-domain and wavelength-domain peaks differ by the Planck Jacobian (Wien non-invariance); they are reported separately.
domain_conversion.target_domain: wavelength-domain Wien peak lambda_peak = b / T

primary_metric.interpolation_method: fixed parabolic-vertex refinement of the located raw-bin peak; deterministic sampling-resolution diagnostic, not a model fit

reference_temperature.provenance_role: separately pinned reference temperature (TASK-0815 contract); not a fitted parameter chosen from the FIRAS rows

sampling_resolution.note: FIRAS bin spacing limits the raw-bin peak precision; the parabolic refinement reports the sampling sensitivity.
